lede: A look at September 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Grazia Deledda and Daniel Pudil, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Dawn (spacecraft)
events.desc: NASA launches the Dawn probe to the asteroid belt.

events.title: Google
events.desc: The Google internet search engine retroactively claims this date as its birthday.

events.title: BAC TSR-2
events.desc: The British TSR-2 aircraft XR219 makes its maiden flight.

events.title: SS Patrick Henry
events.desc: The SS Patrick Henry is launched, becoming the first of more than 2,700 Liberty ships.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Tripartite Pact is signed in Berlin by Germany, Japan and Italy.

events.title: Lij Iyasu
events.desc: Iyasu V is proclaimed deposed as ruler of Ethiopia in a palace coup in favor of his aunt Zewditu.

events.title: Ellen Southard
events.desc: The merchant sailing ship Ellen Southard is wrecked in a storm at Liverpool.

events.title: Jean-François Champollion
events.desc: Jean-François Champollion officially informs the Académie des Inscriptions et Belles Lettres in France that he has deciphered the Rosetta Stone.

events.title: Jesuits
events.desc: The Society of Jesus (Jesuits) receives its charter from Pope Paul III.

events.title: William the Conqueror
events.desc: William the Conqueror and his army set sail from the mouth of the Somme river, beginning the Norman conquest of England.

births.name: Grazia Deledda
births.line: Italian novelist and poet, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Dick Advocaat
births.line: Dutch football manager and former player

births.name: Avril Lavigne
births.line: Canadian singer-songwriter, actress, and fashion designer

deaths.text: Julius Wagner-Jauregg, Austrian physician and neuroscientist, Nobel Prize laureate
Maximilian I, Elector of Bavaria, Duke/Elector of Bavaria from 1597 to 1651
